key_insight: Casing eccentricity and weak cement-formation interfaces can dramatically redirect fracture initiation and growth in heterogeneous wellbores, lowering crack-initiation pressure and enabling non-radial failure modes.
engineering_relevance: The study helps predict when wellbores are at higher risk of sustained casing pressure and interface-driven failure, informing casing placement and interface design choices.
method: Hybrid phase-field fracture model for heterogeneous wellbore systems with weak interfaces
Validated against benchmark problems with analytical and numerical reference solutions
Parametric simulations in 2D and 3D to isolate effects of interface strength and casing eccentricity
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical validation plus simulations showing up to a 30% reduction in crack-initiation pressure with eccentricity, and a 3D case revealing depth-dependent crack nucleation and interface coalescence.
watch_item: Results depend on the assumed phase-field parameters, interface strengths, and idealized geometry, so field calibration is needed before operational use.
paper_title: Phase-field analysis of fracture in heterogeneous wellbore systems: effects of casing eccentricity and cement-formation interface strength
